step_by_step_reasoning: Based on the whiteboard and transcript: 1. Multiple client S3 buckets generate access logs. 2. A centralized S3 access logs bucket collects them. 3. This bucket triggers a Lambda function. 4. Lambda parses logs, resolves IP to country using a GeoIP database, and converts it to a numeric country ID. 5. Lambda inserts records into Amazon Elasticsearch Service (represented as OpenSearch). 6. The Elasticsearch anomaly detector identifies issues and triggers SNS alerts to the service team (UserCompanyAgent).

graph.name: Mobilewala Proactive Near Real-Time Anomaly Detector
graph.notes: System processes 18-20M logs/month to detect anomalous access patterns by mapping IPs to numeric IDs for Elasticsearch anomaly detection.

nodes.name: Client Buckets
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Individual client buckets storing shared data.

nodes.name: S3 Access Logs
nodes.notes: Consolidated target bucket receiving all S3 access logs.

nodes.name: Log Parser Lambda
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Processes 18-20M logs/month. Resolves IP to Country ID.

nodes.name: Elasticsearch Service
nodes.notes: Runs Anomaly Detector on custom Country ID numeric field.

nodes.name: SNS
nodes.notes: Triggers email alerts when anomalies are detected.
